title: U.S. Department of Energy and Environmental Protection Agency Announce $6 Million to Support Development of Advanced Biofuels
content: Today, the U.S. Department of Energy's (DOE) Bioenergy Technologies Office (BETO) and the U.S. Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) announced $6 million in funding for three projects that will advance biofuel development and support U.S. leadership in energy and emissions innovation. Funded through the Inflation Reduction Act (IRA), the projects will support research to improve performance and reduce costs of high-impact biofuel production technologies; scale up production systems with industry; and support the U.S bioeconomy.
author: Analysts Account
